title: Central 1 Current Accounts and High Interest Savings Accounts
body_text:
Central 1 Current Accounts and High Interest Savings Accounts
1	Introduction to Central 1 Current Accounts and High Interest Savings Accounts
1.1	Overview of Central 1 Current Accounts and High Interest Savings Accounts
Your financial institution must maintain a Canadian dollar current account with Central 1. The current account is used to settle financial activities and fees related to the products and services that your financial institution uses. Your financial institution can also maintain a US current account with Central 1 to process items in US dollars. If your financial institution doesnât have a US account, US dollar items are rejected.
Central 1 High Interest Savings Accounts (HISAs) are a low-risk deposit investment option for your financial institution. Only authorized users can view your Central 1 account or HISA summary and transaction information, view and export posting account activity, view your account rates, and transfer funds between your accounts in near real-time via the Treasury Connect application. See Treasury Connect User Guide, Form 6444, for more information.
Authorized users can view transactions posted to your financial institutionâs current account(s) in Current Account Services (CAS) Online. Account information is available for each currency type your financial institution maintains with Central 1 (i.e., Canadian dollars, US dollars, Pound Sterling, and Euros). Data is refreshed every 20 to 30 minutes throughout the business day. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
Central 1 no longer accepts deposits in foreign currencies other than USD. However, foreign deposits previously made through Central 1 are available on the Online Return System (ORS) for seven years from the date of deposit. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for procedures on how to view these deposits.
Related Payment Solutions Manual Parts
Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part
File Exchange and Report Distribution Part
Deposit Processing Part
Incoming Clearing Items Part
Online Return System (ORS) Part
1.2	Central 1 Account Interest Calculations and Float Charges
See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for information on how interest on Central 1 accounts is calculated and paid, and how float charges are calculated on foreign currency accounts.
1.3	Service Charges for Central 1 Current Accounts
Fees relating to current accounts are charged to the applicable account (i.e., CAD or USD). For current fees, Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
Other financial institutions can refer to their agreement with Central 1 or contact their Central 1 relationship manager.
1.4	Preparing and Processing Deposits
See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on preparing and processing deposits to your Central 1 current account.
Note:	Central 1 doesnât accept Euro and Pound Sterling deposit items via paper exchange or via Branch Capture or Corporate Capture. Your financial institution needs to develop internal policies for handling Euro and Pound Sterling deposit items. These items can be sent on collection, or you can have the customer request an incoming wire instead.
1.5	Processing Incoming Clearings
Incoming clearing items are drawn directly on your financial institution or on a customer of your financial institution. Once the item is cashed or deposited, it is sent through the clearing to Central 1 and debited to your Central 1 current account.
See the Incoming Clearing Items Part for procedures on processing incoming clearing items.
1.6	Viewing Deposits and Initiating Trace Requests
Use the Online Return System (ORS) to search for, view, and print deposit items and deposit slips. ORS facilitates the return of cheques and AFT transactions. ORS also allows users to view incoming clearing items (including chargebacks), research deposit items (including deposit adjustments), reinstate cheques, and create AFT notice of change transactions. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Use ServiceNow to initiate a trace request in response to an inquiry on a deposit item that canât be resolved via ORS, or when a financial adjustment is required. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on initiating a trace request.
1.7	Roles and Responsibilities for Credit Unions and Central 1
The credit union is responsible for reconciling the current account and advising Central 1 promptly of any errors or omissions.
Central is responsible for:
Posting items daily using predetermined standardized narrative codes. See Section 5.2, Central 1 Transaction and Charge Codes, for more information.
Sending out account statements once per month
Investigating and quickly correcting any errors or omissions brought to its attention. For errors brought to Centralâs attention after 90 days, the credit union is assessed an investigation fee to offset the cost of retrieving records.
1.8	Contacts and Websites
